headline: South Sudan FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — structural continuation. UNICEF: 7.8M in acute food insecurity (56% of population, IPC April-July 2026); 73,300 at Catastrophe IPC Phase 5. 200,000 displaced from Akobo fighting; 100,000 fled to Ethiopia. Disease outbreaks: cholera, malaria, measles. Fangak hospital bombardment (May 3, 7 killed, 20+ injured) documented in floor record. Floor sustained; conduct documentation appended.

conduct_documentation.summary: Structural continuation: 7.8M in acute food insecurity (56% of population). 73,300 at Catastrophe IPC Phase 5. Akobo displacement 200,000. Disease outbreaks ongoing. Fangak hospital bombardment (May 3) anchor event documented in May 11 record.
new_conduct_categories_this_cycle: UNICEF/IPC: 7.8M in acute food insecurity (56% of population); 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe (April-July 2026).
200,000 displaced from Akobo fighting; 100,000 fled to Ethiopia (IRC).
Disease outbreaks: cholera, malaria, measles continue.
OHCHR UN experts call for civilian protection (April anchor); Fangak hospital bombardment (May 3 anchor) — humanitarian-site targeting continues to be a pattern category.
conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Structural humanitarian failure at population scale + active conflict-related displacement + hospital strikes maintains the densest possible floor-conduct evidentiary basis. Floor exit would require sustained cessation of active conflict, restored humanitarian access, and substantial reduction in IPC Phase 5 population.

key_evidence: UNICEF May 2026: 7.8M in acute food insecurity (56% of population); 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe (April-July 2026 IPC).
IRC: 200,000 displaced from Akobo fighting; 100,000 fled to Ethiopia.
OHCHR (April 2026): UN experts urge immediate protection of civilians amid escalating violence.
Fangak hospital bombardment (May 3 anchor): 7 killed, 20+ injured — humanitarian-site targeting pattern.
Disease outbreaks: cholera, malaria, measles continue.

evidence.source: UNICEF — Hunger intensifies South Sudan: 7.8M face high acute food insecurity
evidence.finding: 7.8M in acute food insecurity (56% of population); 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe.

evidence.source: IRC — South Sudan renewed fighting in Akobo state
OHCHR — UN experts urge immediate protection of civilians

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). No effective detection mechanisms for population-scale crisis.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 unchanged (floor). Civilian harm at population scale.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Active conflict; no remedial action.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). Population-scale food insecurity disproportionately affecting marginalized communities.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). Sovereignty claim used while population-scale crisis active.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). No accountability for Fangak hospital strike or related conduct.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 unchanged (floor). Structural failure across food security, displacement, conflict, disease.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). Stated commitments to population welfare contradicted by structural conditions.

negativeSignals: 7.8M in food insecurity; 73,300 at Catastrophe Phase 5; 200,000 displaced Akobo; disease outbreaks; Fangak hospital bombardment anchor.
sector_context: East/Central Africa / structural-humanitarian-crisis cluster. South Sudan structural conditions parallel Sudan (active conflict + humanitarian access blockage + population-scale crisis).
open_actions_resolved: Structural continuation documented for May 12 cycle
open_actions_carried: Watch: any change in IPC Phase 5 population
Watch: any movement on Fangak hospital accountability
methodology_consistency_check: Floor-entity conduct documentation only. Methodologically consistent.
